Midterm Prep

  • Image projection

    • Rotation in homogeneous coordinates: https://chatgpt.com/c/6990b488-f9d4-832b-9fa6-8f6d37428e6c
    • Project and unproject from ps4
  • Convolution flips one of the arguments. Remember the formula
  • kron
    def image_operator_from_sep_kernels(
    img_shape: Tuple[int, int],
    kernel_x: Float[Tensor, "*"],
    kernel_y: Float[Tensor, "*"],
    )  Float[Tensor, "N N"]:
    """
    Constructs a 2D convolution operator for an image by combining separable 1D kernels.

    Args:
        img_shape: Tuple[int, int]
            The (height, width) dimensions of the image.
        kernel_x: Tensor
            The 1D convolution kernel to be applied horizontally.
        kernel_y: Tensor
            The 1D convolution kernel to be applied vertically.

    Returns:
        Tensor of shape (h*w, h*w)
            The 2D convolution operator acting on a flattened image.
    """
    # STUDENT CODE HERE
    conv_operator = torch.kron(matrix_from_convolution_kernel(kernel_y, img_shape[0]), matrix_from_convolution_kernel(kernel_x, img_shape[1]))

    # END STUDENT CODE
    return conv_operator

  • Hi, the exam is still in the works, so nothing is too concrete yet. It will be pen-and-paper though, and will also have questions on both concepts and implementation / algorithmic behaviors. We expect that you understand the topics covered in lecture, concepts included in the PSET, and overall functionality of everything we’ve asked you to implement.

-You will only be tested on content relevant to the problem sets. You do not need to read extra papers unless it’s something you have explicitly implemented as a p-set. But you should understand the material in the slides at the level of detail that is in the slides. See lecture 7 (I think) for the type of questions we have in mind. Essentially, in preparing questions we went through the problem sets. We have have strayed a tiny bit to include background in the slides relevant to a p-set, but the guiding star was always the p-sets.
